Change of Nominated Adviser
Oxford, UK - 17 February 2026 -
Oxford BioDynamics Plc (AIM: OBD, the Company), a precision clinical diagnostics company bringing specific and sensitive tests to the practice of medicine based on its EpiSwitch® 3D genomics platform,
announces that it has today appointed Singer Capital Markets Advisory LLP as Nominated Adviser and Broker to the Company.

Notes to Editors
About Oxford BioDynamics Plc
Oxford BioDynamics Plc (AIM: OBD) is an international biotechnology company, advancing personalized healthcare by developing and commercializing precision clinical diagnostic tests for life-changing diseases.
Currently OBD has two commercially available products: the
EpiSwitch® PSE
(EpiSwitch Prostate Screening test) and
EpiSwitch® CiRT
(Checkpoint Inhibitor Response Test) blood tests. PSE boosts the predictive accuracy of a PSA test from 55% to 94% when testing the presence or absence of prostate cancer. CiRT is a highly accurate (85%) predictive response test to immuno-oncology checkpoint inhibitor treatments.
The tests are based on OBD's proprietary 3D genomic biomarker platform, EpiSwitch® which enables screening, evaluation, validation and monitoring of biomarkers to diagnose patients or determine how individuals might respond to a disease or treatment.
OBD's clinical smart tests have the potential to be used across a broader range of indications, and new tests are being developed in the areas of oncology, neurology, inflammation, hepatology and animal health.
The Group's headquarters and UK laboratories are in Oxford, UK. Its US operations and clinical laboratory are in Maryland, USA, along with a reference laboratory in Penang, Malaysia.
OBD is listed on the London Stock Exchange's AIM (LSE: OBD). For more information, please visit the Company's website,
www.oxfordbiodynamics.com
, X (@OxBioDynamics) or
LinkedIn
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Board Change
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 2529Q
Oxford BioDynamics PLC
26 January 2026
Oxford BioDynamics Plc
(
"
OBD
" or the "
Company
" and, together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
"
)
Board Change
Oxford, UK - 26 January 2026 -
Oxford BioDynamics Plc (AIM: OBD, the Company), a precision clinical diagnostics company bringing specific and sensitive tests to the practice of medicine based on its EpiSwitch® 3D genomics platform,
confirms that Stephen Diggle has stepped down as a Director of the Company with effect from today.
As set out in the announcement of the Company's results on 16 December 2025, a
fter serving as Non-Executive Director for nine years, in line with good practice Steve indicated his intention to stand down from the Board in advance of the AGM to be held today.
Vulpes Investment Management, which is controlled by Steve, remains the Company's largest shareholder through the Vulpes Testudo Fund and the Vulpes Life Sciences Fund, and will nominate a new non-executive director in due course.
Executive Chairman Iain Ross said: "Steve has served on the Board as a Non-Executive Director since October 2016, prior to the Company's listing on AIM later that year. On behalf of the whole Board, I would again like to thank Steve for his support and wisdom over this long period and particularly to me since my appointment early in 2025."
-Ends-

Publication of 2025 Annual Report
Oxford, UK - 22 December 2025 -
Oxford BioDynamics Plc (AIM: OBD, the Company), a precision clinical diagnostics company bringing specific and sensitive tests to the practice of medicine based on its EpiSwitch® 3D genomics platform,
announces
that
its annual report and accounts for the year ended 30 September 2025 ("Annual Report") is available in electronic form on its website at www.oxfordbiodynamics.com/investors/financial-results. Hard copies of the report (incorporating the Notice of Annual General Meeting) will be posted to shareholders today.
As stated in the preliminary results announcement on 16 December 2025, the Company's Annual General Meeting will be held at 3140 Rowan Place, John Smith Drive, Oxford Business Park South, Oxford, OX4 2WB, UK on 26 January 2026 at 11.00 am.
-Ends-

company_30s.what_they_do: Oxford BioDynamics makes blood‑based epigenetic tests that flag early signs of cancer and other diseases.  Their flagship platform, EpiSwitch, turns a simple blood draw into a rapid, non‑invasive screening tool.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of precision diagnostics and big data.  Early cancer detection is a huge unmet need, and NHS plans for nationwide prostate cancer screening give a massive addressable market.  Partnerships with Google Cloud, Bupa UK and Pfizer give credibility and a path to commercialisation.
company_30s.current_state: OBD is still in the development‑to‑commercialisation phase.  It runs a series of clinical trials, is expanding its partner network, and is burning cash at a high rate.  The stock is trading at a very low price that reflects its pre‑revenue status, but the company is steadily moving toward regulatory milestones.

rns_narrative: The first theme is clinical validation: a 5‑year NCI‑sponsored trial in the U.S. and a new test for chronic fatigue are underway.  These studies aim to prove the technology’s utility beyond prostate cancer.
The second theme is partnership acceleration: agreements with Google Cloud, Bupa UK, and Pfizer signal a push toward commercialization and larger market reach.
The third theme is corporate housekeeping: board changes, AGM results, and share‑option deals indicate the company is keeping governance tight while preparing for the next funding round.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet buzz around NHS screening, with traders largely ignoring the stock.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some investors see the NHS partnership as a launchpad for a mass‑screening rollout and are quietly backing the company.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to heavy cash burn, lack of revenue, and the risk that the company could be delisted if it fails to meet milestones.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has moved from flat neutral chatter to a faint but growing optimism as NHS‑related news surfaces.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for OBD is flat – no public search activity, which is a signal of maximum surprise potential when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the Q4 2025 interim results – a positive earnings or cash‑flow update could lift the stock.
Official NHS approval or a formal agreement to roll out the prostate‑cancer screening program.
Completion of the NCI trial or a significant milestone announcement from the U.S. study.
A partnership announcement with a major pharma or diagnostics firm that expands distribution.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in volume and a break below the 52‑week low support level.  Also monitor the AGM minutes for any new capital‑raising plans or changes to the board that might affect risk.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is deep in a crash‑recovery cycle – it has already capitulated and is now waiting for a catalyst.  The APEX compression score suggests a tight range that could explode once a trigger appears.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A deep crash of 70‑95% creates a huge potential upside if the price rebounds to even half of its pre‑crash level – that is a 100‑200% gain.  The NHS screening news and multiple clinical trials provide a credible path to mean reversion, and the company’s partnerships give it a realistic commercialization route.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure NHS approval or if the clinical trials produce negative results, the stock could stay mired.  Persistent cash burn without revenue and the risk of a delisting due to regulatory or financial shortfalls are real threats.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200% (recovery to 50% of pre‑crash value).  Potential downside: limited to the remaining 30% of the current price, because the stock is already a deep bottom.  The asymmetry is roughly 1:2, favouring a long position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike and a break of the 52‑week low support.  Size the position at 5‑10% of the portfolio and set a stop 30% below entry to protect against a lingering crash.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash and monitor for any NHS‑related announcement or a positive trial milestone.  If either occurs, consider a quick entry with the same risk parameters.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the company has no immediate delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash stock with NHS screening on the horizon offers a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
